heading: Trial work periods and vocational rehabilitation for certain veterans with total disability ratings

Ratification of Actions of Secretary of Veterans Affairs During Lapsed Period, , , provided that:Pub. L. 102–291, § 2(e)106 Stat. 178May 20, 1992“The following actions of the Secretary of Veterans Affairs during the period beginning on , and ending on the date of the enactment of this Act [] are hereby ratified with respect to that period:February 1, 1992May 20, 1992“(1)A failure to reduce the disability rating of a veteran who began to engage in a substantially gainful occupation during that period.“(2)The provision of a vocational training program (including related evaluations and other related services) to a veteran under , United States Code, and the making of related determinations under that section.section 1524 of title 38“(3)The provision of health care and services to a veteran pursuant to , United States Code.”section 1525 of title 38

Information; Temporary Program; Administrator, , , directed Administrator of Veterans’ Affairs to provide, not later than , to certain veterans with service-connected disabilities, a statement containing information explaining subsec. (b) of this section, information explaining purposes and availability of and eligibility for, and procedures for pursuing, a vocational rehabilitation program under chapter 31 of this title, and a summary description of scope of services and assistance available under chapter 31.Pub. children.text: The disability rating of a qualified veteran who begins to engage in a substantially gainful occupation after , may not be reduced on the basis of the veteran having secured and followed a substantially gainful occupation unless the veteran maintains such an occupation for a period of 12 consecutive months.January 31, 1985

For purposes of this section, the term “qualified veteran” means a veteran who has a service-connected disability, or service-connected disabilities, not rated as total but who has been awarded a rating of total disability by reason of inability to secure or follow a substantially gainful occupation as a result of such disability or disabilities.

children.label: b
children.text: The Secretary shall make counseling services described in and placement and postplacement services described in available to each qualified veteran (whether or not the veteran is participating in a vocational rehabilitation program under chapter 31 of this title).section 3104(a)(2) of this titlesection 3104(a)(5) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: notice of the provisions of this section;

children.label: B
children.text: information explaining the purposes and availability of and eligibility for, and the procedures for pursuing, a vocational rehabilitation program under chapter 31 of this title; and

children.label: C
children.text: a summary description of the scope of services and assistance available under that chapter.
After providing the notice required under paragraph (1) of this subsection, the Secretary shall offer the veteran the opportunity for an evaluation under .section 3106(a) of this title
